part.text:
You are working in an environment with SkyPilot installed.

Create a SkyPilot task YAML at `/home/user/private_dep_task.yaml` that trains a model using a **private GitHub repository** as a dependency. The private repo is hosted at `github.com/my-org/private-model-lib` and requires authentication.

The task must:
- Be named `private-model-training`
- Target GCP with instance type `n1-standard-4` (no GPU needed)
- Declare an environment variable `GIT_TOKEN` in the `envs` section (with a placeholder value of `your-token-here` as default)
- In the `setup` command: clone the private repo using the token (`https://${GIT_TOKEN}@github.com/my-org/private-model-lib.git`) and install it with `pip install -e ./private-model-lib`
- In the `run` command: execute `python3 -c "import private_model_lib; print('Model lib loaded successfully')"`

After writing the YAML, validate it can be parsed by SkyPilot:
```
python3 -c "import sky; t = sky.Task.from_yaml('/home/user/private_dep_task.yaml'); print('Task:', t.name)"
```
This must exit 0 and print `Task: private-model-training`.

Also write a configuration report to `/home/user/task_report.json` with:
- `task_name`: `"private-model-training"`
- `cloud`: `"gcp"`
- `instance_type`: `"n1-standard-4"`
- `uses_private_repo`: `true`
- `env_vars`: list of declared environment variable names (e.g., `["GIT_TOKEN"]`)

Successfully created the SkyPilot task YAML and configuration report:

**Task YAML created at:** `/home/user/private_dep_task.yaml`

The task includes:
- Name: `private-model-training`
- Cloud: GCP with instance type `n1-standard-4`
- Environment variable `GIT_TOKEN` with placeholder value `your-token-here`
- Setup command: clones private repo using token and installs with pip
- Run command: imports and tests the private model library

**Validation passed:** The YAML was successfully parsed by SkyPilot and printed `Task: private-model-training`

**Configuration report created at:** `/home/user/task_report.json`

The report contains:
```json
{
  "task_name": "private-model-training",
  "cloud": "gcp",
  "instance_type": "n1-standard-4",
  "uses_private_repo": true,
  "env_vars": ["GIT_TOKEN"]
}
```

The task is ready to use. Users will need to set the `GIT_TOKEN` environment variable to their actual GitHub personal access token when running the task to authenticate access to the private repository.
